articles.title: Say goodbye to the stubborn sticker residue with these easy steps

articles.title: Brent crude oil crosses $100: US-Iran tensions, blocked supply route push prices higher
articles.description: Brent crude oil prices crossed $100 as tensions between the United States and Iran increased. Peace talks are uncertain and the Strait of Hormuz is mostly blocked, affecting global oil supply. This situation is creating fear of shortage in energy markets. Rising conflict risks and supply disruption are the main reasons behind the sudden increase in crude oil prices globally.

articles.title: Are you accidentally damaging your TV every time you clean it?
articles.description: Protect your TV screen from damage by ditching paper towels and harsh cleaners. A soft, lint-free microfiber cloth is your best bet for everyday dust and smudges. For tougher dirt, use a damp cloth with distilled water or a tiny drop of detergent. Always apply liquids to the cloth, not the screen, and wipe gently to maintain picture quality.

articles.title: How your slicing style is silently ruining your fresh bread
articles.description: Stale bread is a common kitchen woe, but science reveals a simple solution: how you slice it. Proper cutting, especially with a sharp knife, preserves the crust's protective barrier, preventing rapid moisture loss. Slicing from the middle and rejoining halves traps moisture, extending freshness. Understanding starch retrogradation is key to enjoying your artisanal loaves longer.

articles.title: RBI brings cross border transactions under e-mandate rules, allows customer to opt out anytime
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India is enhancing digital fraud prevention. Cross-border recurring electronic payments via cards, prepaid instruments, and UPI are now under E-mandate rules. Customers can opt-out of transactions or mandates. Limits are set for recurring payments. Pre-transaction notifications are mandatory for most debits. These rules aim to protect customers and ensure secure electronic transactions across borders.

articles.title: Pak extends airspace ban for Indian flights
articles.description: Islamabad: Pakistan on Tuesday further extended the ban on Indian aircraft from using its airspace for another month.Pakistan has kept its airspace shut to Indian flights since April 24, 2025, a move that reportedly caused a loss of billions of rupees to the Indian airlines.The Pakistan Airports Authority said in the Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) that "all Indian-registered, leased, commercial and military aircraft will remain barred from Pakistani airspace until 5 am on May 24, 2026".The notice was issued days before the previous one was set to expire on April 24.Pakistan closed its airspace for Indian airlines following tension in the wake of the Pahalgam terror attack, for which New Delhi blamed Islamabad.Pakistan, however, rejected the allegations and also called for a transparent inquiry.India, in response, also closed its airspace to Pakistani airlines. Pakistan has imposed such restrictions on India for the first time.The ban has hit commercial Indian airlines badly as they used Pakistani airspace to operate most of their flights to and from different Indian cities to Gulf countries in particular.Airspace closures were previously enacted during the 1999 Kargil conflict and the 2019 Pulwama crisis.

articles.title: SHOCKING FOOTAGE! US Gunner 'TAKES AIM' As HUGE Iran Tanker RAMS Towards Kharg In BIG GULF SHOWDOWN
articles.description: A high-stakes faceoff is looming in the Gulf. An Iranian supertanker is on course for Kharg Island and US warships are standing in its path. According to Iranian reports, a Very Large Crude Carrier, or VLCC, has crossed what’s being described as a US “siege line”, re-entering Iranian waters after completing a massive oil transfer. Now, the vessel is expected to dock at Kharg Island, a key Iranian oil terminal. If confirmed, this would mark a major breach in Washington’s effort to choke Tehran’s oil exports. New footage purportedly shows a US military helicopter, a gunner aiming directly at a cargo ship, issuing a stern warning. Watch the video for more details.

articles.title: Bombay high court dismisses PIL seeking recovery of Mohan Bhagwat’s Z-plus security cost; questions plea’s intent
articles.description: The Bombay High Court has dismissed a Public Interest Litigation demanding recovery of Z-plus security costs for RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat. The petitioner cited a Supreme Court ruling on industrialist Mukesh Ambani's security costs being borne by his family.
